publicV1.reason: Repo reads gain additive content-copy status and copy-timestamp fields; task creation gains a new actionable rejection when the selected repo's content copy is not ready (copy missing/refreshing/failed). No existing field changes shape or meaning; refresh and local-path import stay Console-internal endpoints outside the /v1 registry.

mcp.reason: Matching tools inherit the additive repo copy-status fields from the shared contracts and create_task surfaces the same copy-not-ready rejection through the shared task-creation path.

openapi.reason: Additive repo copy-status schema fields and the new task-creation rejection derive from the canonical public registry and contracts.

apiPlayground.reason: The playground derives the affected endpoints and response rendering from the same registry, including the additive fields and the new non-2xx rejection body.

internalOnly.reason: Adds the host-side repo-store bare-mirror copy service (import-time acquisition, atomic completion, manual refresh, delete cascade), the fail-closed local-path import behind CAP_LOCAL_IMPORT_ROOT, the WorkspaceSource union with per-provider injection (aio-local read-only volume-subpath mount, BoxLite streamed uploadArchive tar), the gated legacy git fallback, injection-stage provisioning diagnostics, Console import/refresh/gating UI, and the repo-store compose volume.

protocolDifferences.detail: Idempotency-Key remains an HTTP-only retry contract and is not an MCP argument; its durable admission work item is committed with the winning REST task row.
MCP structured content remains the canonical initial Task while compatibility text retains the historical task handle.
MCP metadata continues to explain immediate asynchronous provisioning and polling without claiming support for the HTTP-only idempotency header.
REST keeps its dedicated task-create throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter.
